message:
feat(agents): implement agent-specific profiles and trust levels (Epic 9)

Add comprehensive agent detection and profile system that allows dcg to
apply different trust levels and configuration overrides based on which
AI coding agent is invoking it (Claude Code, Aider, Continue, Codex CLI,
Gemini CLI, or unknown agents).

Agent Profile Features:
- Trust levels (high/medium/low) for graduated security strictness
- Per-agent pack enable/disable via disabled_packs and extra_packs
- Additional allowlist entries specific to each agent
- Option to disable base allowlist for untrusted agents
- Profile resolution: exact match -> unknown profile -> default profile

Config API additions (src/config.rs):
- enabled_pack_ids_for_agent(): Compute effective packs with agent overrides
- additional_allowlist_for_agent(): Get agent-specific allowlist entries
- allowlist_disabled_for_agent(): Check if allowlists disabled for agent
- trust_level_for_agent(): Get resolved trust level for agent
- Comprehensive test coverage (12 new tests)

Agent detection improvements (src/agent.rs):
- Make is_known() and is_explicit() const fn for compile-time evaluation
- Add AGENT_ENV_VARS constant for test isolation
- Fix test environment variable pollution by saving/restoring all agent
  env vars during tests (prevents ambient env from affecting results)

CLI integration (src/cli.rs):
- Add AgentInfo struct for JSON output with detected agent, trust level,
  and detection method
- Include agent info in test command output (both JSON and verbose modes)
- Show agent detection details at -vv verbosity level

Configuration example:
```toml
[agents.claude-code]
trust_level = "high"
additional_allowlist = ["npm run build"]

[agents.unknown]
trust_level = "low"
extra_packs = ["paranoid"]
```

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
